rulings.Case Title: J.R. MARKETING, LLC., A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al VS. HARTFORD CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Prevent Entry Of Default And Default Judgment Against Kanen Processing And Wheatland Baking, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 12, 2012, Line 13, PLAINTIFFS J.R. MARKETING, LLC., JANE RATTO, ROBERT RATTO, PENELOPE KANE, LENORE DEMARTINIS, GERMAIN DEMARTINIS' Motion To Prevent Entry Of Default And Default Judgment Against Kanen Processing And Wheatland Baking, Inc. Granted in part. Until further order, no default may be entered against Kane Processing, LLC and Wheatland Baking, Inc. on the cross-complaint. The motion is continued to May 14, 2012, to allow Kane Processing, LLC and Wheatland Baking, Inc. to pay their taxes and obtain certificates of revivor. If they do not obtain the certificates by that time, then the Court will allow the entry of the defaults. Entry of any default judgment shall occur only after the cross-complaint is adjudicated on the merits. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: YUE YING WANG VS. NATURAL WONDER A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-11-506931 WITH CASE CGC-09-492132

rulings.Case Title: ELIJAH EFANOV, BY AND THROUGH HIS GUARDIAN AD VS. DYNAMO INDUSTRIES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 12, 2012, Line 14, DEFENDANT YMCA OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement. Dynamo Industries' application for determination good faith settlement is denied. Dynamo Indusries did not satisfy its burden of presenting competent non-conclusory evidence of all the Tech-Bilt factors. In particular, apart from past medical bills, the only purpoted evidence of the amount of plaintiff's damages and Dynamo Industries' liability for any or all of those damages is the declaraton of Dynamo Industries' counesl, which is insufficient. (Weil & Brown, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial 12:872-73, citing, inter alia, Greshko v. County of Los Angeles (1987) 194 Cal. App 3d 822). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: HERMANA GRANT VS. MICHAEL FREEMAN-MCGUIRE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Allow Personal Representative Of Estate To Continue Litigation; Declaration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 12, 2012, Line 5, PLAINTIFF HERMANA GRANT'S Motion To Allow Personal Representative Of Estate To Continue Litigation. Granted. The motion is supported by good cause and none of the arguments made by defendant negates the fact that what is sought by the motion is a routine procedure. Any arguments regarding whether Annette Shelton can properly pursue any of the claims or any of the claims are otherwise deficient are best addressed by a motion of judgment on the pleadings or a summary motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: HERMANA GRANT VS. MICHAEL FREEMAN-MCGUIRE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Judgmnt On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 12, 2012, Line 5, DEFENDANT MICHAEL FREEMAN-MCGUIRE Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Granted in part and denied in part. The motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted without leave to amend as to the first cause of action and denied as to all other causes of action. The first cause of action for quiet title relates only to determination of Plaintiff's life estate interest, which extinguished on Hermana Grant's death. All other causes of action are adequately pled and survive Hermana Grant's death. Defendant's standing argument fails in light of the ruling on Plaintiff's Motion to Allow Personal Representative to Continue Litigation. The motion to expunge lis pendens is granted. Once the quiet title claim is removed from the case, Plaintiff no longer has a viable real property claim. The motion for attorney fees is denied. Plaintiff and her counsel acted with substantial justification and imposition of attorney fees under the circumstances would be unjust. Defendant's request for a certified copy of Plaintiff's Death Certificate is denied. There is no authority supporting such a request. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION INC DBA GREAT VS. NIMRATAMOL DOSANJH, AKA NIMRAT AMOL DOSANJH, AKA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Right To Attach Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 12, 2012, Line 2, PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION INC DBA GREAT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Right To Attach Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. Denied, without prejudice. No opposition was filed, but the moving party failed to provide courtesy copies of the moving papers per San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests, and must bring a proposed order to the hearing. In the event no party contests the tentative ruling, the moving party may, in lieu of appearing at the hearing, prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and email it to ernst@ernsthalperin.com. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: FRONTIER RENEWABLES LLC, VS. GP JOULE KONZEPT GMBH & CO KG A GERMAN CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Application And Application For Preliminary Injunction; Memo Of P & A; Req. For Judicial Ntc And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 12, 2012, Line 12, DEFENDANT GP JOULE KONZEPT GMBH & CO KG A GERMAN CORPORATION'S Application For Preliminary Injunction. Granted in part. Subsections (a) and (c) of defendant's proposed order are granted and subsections (b) and (d) are denied. Subsections (a) and (c) flow from the rights granted to defendant in article 7 of the parties' contract. Subsections (b) and (d) go beyond the terms of the parties' contract. However, plaintiff may seek leave to modify this injunction to permit the sale and/or encumbrance of any of the "collateral" if it can show that the sale or encumbrance does not impair the value of the collateral to defendant and/or the proceeds of the sale or encumbrance will be applied toward payment of the loan made by defendant to plaintiff. The foregoing is dependent on defendant posting a bond in the amount of $100,000 no later than March 23, 2012. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
